mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate keeps the discount rate anchored and supports equity valuation multiples, so SPX forward returns over the next month are positive when policy is not tightening. If rates rise, the mechanism weakens because the valuation channel reverses.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered by low or falling FEDFUNDS, SPX 20d forward returns are negative in more than 55% of cases or the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in FEDFUNDS above a restrictive threshold tightens financial conditions, raises discount rates, and compresses equity multiples, producing negative SPX returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when the policy rate is already above its recent cycle average.
failure_condition: Reject if, after FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.5%, SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances or the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and demand, so equity prices decline over the next month as earnings expectations are marked down. The signal is directional only when unemployment is increasing rather than merely elevated.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX forward returns after a 0.3pp/3m UNRATE rise are positive in more than 60% of cases or the signal loses significance once the unemployment rise is observed.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth is a high-frequency demand proxy: stronger PAYEMS growth indicates resilient labor income and supports consumer spending, which lifts SPX returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should vanish when payroll growth is negative because income momentum is then deteriorating.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS momentum fails to predict positive 20d SPX returns, or if the sign flips and negative payroll growth is associated with higher forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both strengthen, retail demand and housing activity are jointly improving, indicating broad private-sector spending momentum that should lift SPX over the next quarter. Either series alone is insufficient; the signal requires both demand gauges to be above recent trend.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-HOUST strength does not improve 60d SPX returns versus single-series triggers, or if the interaction term is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates real output is growing without an inflation shock, which supports margins and earnings revisions, producing positive SPX returns over the next month. The mechanism fails when inflation accelerates alongside industrial production because nominal policy pressure offsets the growth benefit.
failure_condition: Reject if higher INDPRO does not outperform when CPIAUCSL is flat-to-down, or if the interaction between output growth and inflation does not materially improve 20d predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 signals weaker aggregate demand and lower future earnings, so SPX should underperform over the next quarter. This is a slower-moving signal that works best when GDP growth is decelerating rather than simply volatile.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns negative versus prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if negative quarterly GDPC1 growth is followed by positive 60d SPX returns in a majority of instances or if the signal fails to beat a random benchmark out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in RSAFS and HOUST marks a demand recession in consumer spending and housing, which should depress SPX returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when both series fall below their recent trend, not when only one weakens.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both fall below their 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST do not coincide with weaker 20d SPX returns, or if the joint signal is weaker than either series alone across rolling validation windows.
